title: ambient-capture.v1 — a best-effort local record that a check ran
description: One record per time a local check fires while you work, appended to a private, regenerable cache by the hook that runs checks (that writer arrives in a later slice). The self-monitoring system OWNS this record's shape and where the cache lives; the hook only writes into it. It is deliberately NOT a finding — it just records that a named check ran and whether it passed, plus a couple of details derived from the moment it ran (what it touched, and when). This capture is best-effort and never complete — local runs can be skipped, and some hooks can't name a target — so nothing in the engine treats it as a guarantee: anything that must be reliable is read from the project's own checked-in record of passed checks, or tracked as its own item, never assumed from this cache. This file is reference data only; its own correctness as a schema is checked automatically.

rule_id.description: The id of the check that ran — the same signal identity the self-monitoring groups by.

outcome.description: Whether the check passed or failed this time. This is read from what the hook reported about the check, not from the hook program's own exit code — the hook relays the check's verdict, it does not decide it.

target.description: What the check ran over this time — the file or context the run touched — or null when the moment names none. This is per-occurrence detail, kept for context only; it is never used to group repeats.

observed_at.description: When the run was seen, written as a UTC moment ending in Z (for example 2026-06-02T14:30:00Z). The trailing-Z UTC shape is what is actually enforced (the date-time format is only a hint).
